_about: Per-crew LLM routing. Copy to llm_providers.json (gitignored) to use it. 'profiles' are named provider chains; 'crews' maps an agent_name to a profile; every other crew uses 'default'. So content crews route to one model (grok) and code crews to another (a real coder). get_llm() tries each provider in the chosen profile top-to-bottom, and within a provider its models in order, falling through on ANY error (across providers). Delete the file to fall back to env (OPENROUTER_MODEL).

_fields.default: profile name used by any crew not listed in 'crews'
_fields.profiles: name -> { providers:[...] }. A provider: {name, type, api_key_env?, base_url?, context?, models:[...], enabled?}
_fields.crews: agent_name -> profile name (the agent_name from `aimeat connect add --agent ...`)
_fields.type: openrouter | ollama (local, keyless) | xai | openai | nvidia (NVIDIA NIM @ build.nvidia.com — free, OpenAI-compatible, ~40 req/min) | generic (decides the litellm prefix + default base_url)
_fields.api_key_env: name of the .env var holding the key (omit for keyless local ollama); a provider whose key is missing is skipped, not fatal
_fields.models: models IN PRIORITY ORDER, each an id string or {id, context}. id has NO litellm prefix. context = that model's MAX context window (tokens) so CrewAI never over-fills a small model.
_fields.enabled: optional false to keep a provider in the file but skip it

content._use: news / editorial / features / research — grok is strong at prose; free + local fall back if grok dies.

coding._use: app / cortex / extension / test crews. Do NOT put grok here (weak at code). Slot your strongest coder as the FIRST provider — e.g. NVIDIA NIM's free frontier coders (glm-5.2 / kimi-k2.6 / minimax-m3), a paid model via OpenRouter, or a local code model.
